name: Mongolia
headline: Mongolia ROTATION-BACKFILL CONFIRMATION at 48.4 / Functional. First-baseline check against canonical INDEX. Mixed-signal cycle: East Asia Forum January 2026 finding that Mongolia 'slipped into electoral autocracy category amid growing concerns over free speech and arbitrary arrests of journalists.' Counter-signal: Five-Year Development Guidelines (2026-2030) submitted to Parliament Oct 30 with human-development + governance pillars; shift from equity-heavy to royalty-based mining agreements (predictability/reduced government project involvement). Net: sustained Functional at 48.4 with elevated AWR/ACC watch (press-freedom + journalist-arrest signals). Sub-threshold negative; no apply.

AWR.rationale: Sustained. East Asia Forum press-freedom concerns are sub-threshold; first-baseline anchor preserved.

ACT.rationale: Sustained. Five-Year Development Guidelines (2026-2030) with human-development pillar is positive infrastructure signal but pre-enactment.

EQU.rationale: Sustained. Mining royalty-based shift may improve resource-equity distribution but pre-implementation.

ACC.rationale: Sustained at first-baseline. Electoral-autocracy backsliding + arbitrary arrests of journalists is concerning but sub-threshold (no new dimensional downgrade absent specific case-count documentation).

evidence.finding: Mongolia slipped into 'electoral autocracy' category amid growing concerns over free speech and arbitrary arrests of journalists.
Mongolia retains democratic framework with regular elections, active political pluralism, and broad societal support for democratic norms — but stability slipping.
Five-Year Development Guidelines (2026-2030) submitted to Parliament Oct 30; eight policy areas including human development, governance, economic diversification.
Shift from equity-heavy to royalty-based mining agreements (predictability + reduced government project involvement).

keyEvidence: East Asia Forum: 'electoral autocracy' backsliding (sub-threshold concern).
Five-Year Development Guidelines (2026-2030) with human-development pillar.
Sub-threshold negative; no apply; AWR/ACC watch elevated.
rationale: Rotation-backfill first-baseline confirmation at 48.4. Mixed-signal cycle. The electoral-autocracy backsliding (East Asia Forum January 2026) is a real AWR/ACC concern but sub-threshold absent specific journalist-arrest case-counts or codified press-restriction legislation. The positive infrastructure signals (Five-Year Guidelines, mining royalty shift) are pre-enactment. Conservative-direction protocol: hold first-baseline at 48.4 with watch. Math: (3+3+3+2.75+3+2.75+3+3)/8 = 23.5/8 = 2.9375 → ((2.9375-1)/4)*100 = 48.44 → 48.4.
whyHeadline: Mongolia first-baseline confirmed at 48.4 (Functional); electoral-autocracy backsliding sub-threshold, watch elevated.

dominantDimension.note: ACC carries the watch-elevation signal (arbitrary journalist arrests + electoral backsliding).

nextForwardSignal.label: Five-Year Guidelines parliamentary passage; specific press-freedom case documentation

boundaryWatchResolution: Not boundary-sensitive. 48.4 sits 7.4pt above Developing/Functional floor.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
